questions.question: Consider a dataset with two continuous attributes, 'age' and 'salary', and a binary target variable 'loan_approved'. Apply the Naïve Bayes algorithm to this dataset and explain how the choice of probability distributions affects the classification accuracy. (10 marks)

A Naïve Bayes classifier is used to predict the 'grade' of a student based on their 'gpa' and 'hours_of_study' attributes. The prior probabilities of the classes are assumed to be uniform, and the probability distributions of the attributes are assumed to be Gaussian. What assumptions do you make about the relationship between the attributes and the target variable? (8 marks)

For a Naïve Bayes classifier with a binary target variable 'spam' and two continuous attributes 'word_count' and 'word_length', what is the probability distribution of the target variable that is commonly assumed? Explain your answer. (5 marks)
